Review: Hokkaido don is a real disappointment. RM82 is not cheap. But SO MUCH rice and very little sashimi and uni (sea urchin). Sashimi-type of scallop should be sweet tasting but the scallops they have, totally TASTELESS. First time tasting such a tasteless sashimi. No freshness. No taste. Sashimi salmon was average. Uni is little until very pathetic, but taste of it is ok. Just too little for this pricing. Disappointment.Ordered their nabeyaki udon too. It came with pork shabu which is not bad. Usually it comes chicken but this place dont put chicken in. They put pork shabu slices.Their yusheng for chinese new year is not bad. Maybe cause the sweet sauce with the yusheng ingredients...Chawanmushi is pricey too. RM12 for a normal small portion with very usual ingredients inside.Sanma fish too fishy taste too.Food: 3/5  |  Service: 3/5  |  Atmosphere: 3/5Group sizeSuitable for all group sizesWait timeNo wait

Review: Ichi Zen isn’t new to me, yet I’ve never thought of leaving a review—until today. Here’s what I ordered:.### 🍲 **Sukiyaki Set (RM28)**The beef slices were decent, the broth was hot, and dipping the meat in cooked it instantly. But here’s the real question—should the raw egg go into the soup or onto the rice? Honestly, I wasn’t sure, but does it really matter? As long as it tastes good, that’s what counts..### 🍊 **Vita C Booster (RM14.80)**Refreshing, zesty, and left me wanting more. A great pick-me-up..### ☕ **Americano (RM8.80)**It was okay—nothing to shout about, but then again, this isn’t a specialty coffee house..### 💧 **Drinking Water (RM1)**A bit surprising to see this on the bill. I personally feel a complimentary glass would be a nice touch, especially when switching between juice and coffee to reset the palate..#### ⭐ **Room for Improvement?**A few tiny flies were hovering around—not a major issue, but noticeable. Could be due to the lingering seafood aroma in the air. Perhaps better ventilation or pest control measures could help..### **Would I return?**Yes, whenever I crave a satisfying Japanese meal..### **Would I recommend it?**Absolutely. The restaurant carries a classic charm, and the ambience makes it a pleasant dining spot...JSE@20250331Food: 5/5  |  Service: 5/5  |  Atmosphere: 3/5Parking spacePlenty of parkingParking optionsPaid parking lot, Paid street parking

The Pork Broth Shabu Shabu was rich and satisfying, with fresh ingredients. Review: Dropped by with my parents to Ichi Zen Restaurant after church today to get a Japanese food fix. It was quite serene inside the restaurant, and very clean.We ordered 3 main dishes, with 4 pieces of sushi.1. Gyokai Yakimeshi - Seafood fried rice, it was really good. It has a sufficient amount of seafood pieces like scallops and prawns to compliment the rice. The serving is just nice for 1 person and costed RM14.2. Tori Momo Esunikku yaki Curry Rice - Rice served with a ladle full of curry with chunks of vegetables, slices of roasted marinated chicken, and a bowl of miso soup. It was a large amount, maybe sufficient for 2 people, it tasted as it should, silky smooth curry gravy which wasn't spicy and a little sweet, and the chicken pieces were juicy. It costed RM29, quite pricey in my opinion.3. Una Salmon Volcano Don - I think this was one of their speciality, because it was delicious. Sushi rice with cucumber, egg and pickled radish I think in the centre, then topped off with slices of salmon belly and unagi (eel), and a half cooked egg which just binds the whole dish together. It had a few pops that looked like salmon roe, but it had a soy sauce taste in it. Some chemistry action there. Quite pricey this one but worth it - RM35.4. Four random sushi pieces - Salmon Yuzu Aburi Sushi (RM4), Hana Ebiko Sushi (RM8), Inari Chuka Idako Sushi (RM5), and California Salmon Belly Sushi (RM6). We enjoyed all of them with a good amount of wasabi.We had hot green tea which was bottomless, to compliment all the dishes.Overall, we enjoyed our experience there, and surely will visit again soon.Food: 5/5  |  Service: 4/5  |  Atmosphere: 5/5Kid-friendlinessI saw a family that requested a baby seat and plate, and they had it.

Review: Melaka has wazen, ichizen and senjutei, which are the holy trinity of japanese restaurants. These types of japanese restaurant you should know what you will be paying. So price aside, i think they have their 'specialty' food which has the truffle sauce thing. You can try it in the sushi/alacartes/noodle. I would highly recommend to try some of that and others are all on standard. Disappointing food for me is the katsu mentai don iirc, not good. The serving time is relatively fast so a big plus for me. Use public bank card got discount. Recommend to try this place at least once or twice.Food: 4/5  |  Service: 5/5  |  Atmosphere: 4/5

Review: This is a Japanese restaurant located Syahbandar area of Melaka.It is a fairly large restaurant.  The interior setup is nicely done.  A nice atmosphere to have a nice Japanese meal.The food preparation standard here is good.  Food especially the Sashimi are fresh.  The menu is reasonably wide.  Food presentations are good too.Pork dishes are served here too.Food portions are acceptable and ideal.The service is good, fast and professional.Cleanliness is good.Public washrooms are available within the restaurant.Prices are reasonable.  Cash, e-wallet and credit card payments are accepted here.Parkings are roadside parkings.  There are numerous shops, restaurants and offices nearby, as a result; during peak periods, getting a parking lot may require some patience.Handicapped patrons can patronize this restaurant easily.11-06-2023 : The Udon and Fried Fish were fantastic.  Both are recommended.Food: 5/5  |  Service: 5/5  |  Atmosphere: 5/5

Review: We are back in melaka after 3 years and visited this place for dinner on Sat evenin. Walled in around 615pm and settled comfortably at counter since its only 2 of us. Most of the table seats had reservations.The menu was extensive and easy to read. We placed our order with the wait staff by reference to the letters and numbers tag to the item. We ate the following:Tuna sashimiBaked oysters in cheese and mushroomShishamo grilledClams in garlic butter gravy4 pcs salmon roll in truffle flavour dressingGrilled beef with inoki mushroomsThe tuna sashimi was fresh and the slices were thick. Everything else (cooked items) we ordered tasted good. The service staff were all friendly and helpful, answering our queries patiently.  We had an enjoyable dinner and hope to be back the next time we are in melaka.Adding pics from our second visit in March 2023.

Review: Love the place. The freshest sashimi in town. The ambience was great. Was our second visit in a month. First visit we could see that they sanitized every table after the customer left. This gave us reassurance to come back the second time. However we did not see this practice anymore during the second visit. MANAGEMENT, hope you can continue the sanitizing practise.The food was superb. Go for it if you love Japanese. Some items on the menu are not found in other places. Do take note the price is on a very high side. Took us more than RM1k for both visits in total. Overall a great place to experience fresh Japanese on special occasions. Not for your daily meals.

Overall a great place to experience fresh Japanese on special occasions. Not for your daily meals.

Review: Came here for the first time on 10th August (yesterday) after seeing that so many local Melaka favourites were all closed (and also since this is a 3 minute walk away from Swan Garden Hotel).Staff was frendly with good service and greeted our family in the Japanese style (of course, this is a Japanese restaurant after all)My meal consisted of (Nigiri Sushi) 2 Unagi, 2 Tamago and 2 Inari, as well as the Unagi Don, Chawan Mushi and Miso Soup. While it is a bit more expensive than most Japanese chain restaurants, the taste is well worth the price! I enjoyed every moment of the meal.One thing that consistently caught my attention was the Studio Ghibli piano music playing over the speakers. I heard Carrying You, Name of Life, Town with an Ocean View, Path of the Wind, as well as songs from other Ghibli movies I haven't seen yet. Being a huge Ghibli fan myself, it greatly lifted my mood (but this does not make my review biased). I made sure to heap praise (about the music) upon the staff at the counter! LOLGiven the chance I would definitely come back (which is unfortunately unlikely since I live in KL!)
